other_observers: first by myself and later with Maggie Boswell and Sandy Arnesson and two others who I don't know.; Glenn Walbek found the bird on the 7th of September
description: This was a tiny gull, with very short thin, black bill, white forehead, dark cap, white face except for dark smudge in front of eye, the dark eye, and a dark auricular patch. The white extended to the sides of the neck where on the dorsal surface there was a dark collar that extended down to the sides of the white brest. While sitting on the water the back was mostly dark but I could see white mottling here and there. In flight, I could see a broad dark M patern formed from the wing tips to the bird's wrist, down the secondaries to the lower back contrasting with a litter broad triangle of lighter coloration posterior to the M. The tail was mostly white but in flight when the bird banked I could see a dark tail tip. The undersides of the wings in flight were light but dar at the wing tips.Behaviors: Spend equal time flying and sitting on the water. When it sat it was always by itself. In flight it was mostly with Sabine's Gulls, one adult a few juveniles with which it was noticeabley smaller and the wings darker. On occasion, it banked and came down to the water to investigate sometimes pulling up and continuing its flight in the middle of the reservoir and sometimes landing.Call: nonePlumage: junenile
